Wrapped each affected label in quotes so Mermaid treats the leading slash as literal content. No semantic change to the workflow. Keystone for fase 5 (parser-canonical user_journey reaches MASS via --upgrade) and forces downstream tooling parsing the manifest to handle the new section. v2.10.2: fix(factory-sync) refine greenfield detection to recognise the legitimate `framework-installed but instantiation-pending` state. Old logic flagged any target with `CLAUDE.md` OR `docs/project_log/` and missing `docs/setup.md` as suspicious materialized-with-lost-setup, requiring `--greenfield` flag. False-positive in the common case where a previous factory-sync seeded skills/hooks/CLAUDE.md on a target before /setup ran (Nexus-Tech-Link surfaced this). New detection: `docs/setup.md` absent → check whether `docs/spec/` holds feature directories. If yes → genuinely suspicious (post-instantiation work in progress, setup.md lost mid-flight), error unless `--greenfield`. If no → legitimate pre-instantiation state, auto-greenfield. Header USAGE block updated to match. Bump: framework_core.scripts/factory-sync.sh 1.5.1 → 1.5.2 (PATCH — bug fix in detection contract, no new flags or behaviour for fully-instantiated targets). v2.10.1: fix(EVOL-025) self-review of PR #16 produced 3 mitigations applied during review (first end-to-end test of Phase 5.5). (1) preflight.sh JSON-output heredoc: `os.environ.get('PRE_FF', '$FINDINGS_FILE')` passed FINDINGS_FILE via bash expansion as a python string default — paths with spaces would have broken the literal. Fixed by exporting `PRE_FF="$FINDINGS_FILE"` as env before invoking python and reading via `os.environ['PRE_FF']` only. Heredoc kept unquoted so `$BASE_REF`/`$CURRENT`/`$CHANGED_FILES` continue to expand correctly. (2) SKILL.md Phase 0: added Execution split note clarifying that Steps 0.1–0.5 are agent-side (LLM judgment, runs at review time, emits the marker) and Step 0 inside preflight.sh is script-side (deterministic verification, only reads the marker). The agent is responsible for running Phase 0 before pushing; the push gate fails closed if the marker is absent. (3) SKILL.md Phase 0.5: added defensive `mkdir -p .claude/state/` note — directory is .gitignored and may not exist on a fresh clone or after state cleanup. NOT mitigated (out of scope, separate fix-PR): the `--json` mode `$CHANGED_FILES` multiline injection bug (pre-existing, unrelated to EVOL-025). Bumps: framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/SKILL.md 1.2.0 → 1.2.1 (PATCH — doc clarifications), framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/scripts/preflight.sh 1.1.2 → 1.1.3 (PATCH — defensive bash quoting). v2.10.0: feat(EVOL-025) review-mode mitigation reporting protocol. factory-pr-review SKILL.md gains Phase 5.5 (`Mitigation hot-fix`, `--review` mode only, optional) — reviewer MAY apply mechanical fixes to clear-cut blockers (security regex hit, broken reference, lock-step drift, manifest↔disk drift, placeholder leakage, missing test with obvious pattern) on the same PR branch and record them in `mitigations_applied[]` instead of leaving the finding open. Eligibility criteria (ALL must hold): mechanical fix, clear-cut category, high reviewer confidence, same branch. Original finding entry stays in `blockers[]/important[]` for traceability — author sees both finding and resolution in a single review comment. `decision` SHOULD remain `comment` (not `approve`) when mitigations were applied — author must confirm acceptance before merge. assets/review_template.md gains `mitigations_applied[]` JSON schema + § 'Rules for mitigations_applied' (eligibility + required fields + decision interaction). post_review.py renders new '🔧 Mitigations applied during review' Markdown section after Findings (only when array is non-empty), citing each mitigation's commit_sha + branch + applied_at + description + verified_by. Backfill: framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/scripts/post_review.py 1.1.0 (was untracked since EVOL-024 — pre-existing gap; entry added now since the file is being modified in this commit). v2.9.2: fix(EVOL-025) preflight.sh tally bug pre-existing since v1.0.0 — `BLOCKER_COUNT=$(grep -c PATTERN FILE || echo 0)` concatenates grep's '0' output (when pattern not found) with echo's '0', producing a multi-line value that breaks the subsequent `[[ $BLOCKER_COUNT -gt 0 ]]` arithmetic test with `syntax error in expression`. Surfaced now because v1.1.0 added a code path with 0 findings (governance-sensitive diff + present marker → no blocker added by Step 0 + no other blocker fires). Replaced with `grep PATTERN FILE | wc -l | tr -d ' '` which always returns a clean single-line integer regardless of match count. framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/scripts/preflight.sh 1.1.1 → 1.1.2. v2.9.1: fix(EVOL-025) coherence-audit marker keyed on `branch_sha` only (was `(session_id, branch_sha)`). The first end-to-end push test surfaced that PreToolUse Bash stdin does not reliably carry `.session_id`: hook extracted empty SID, exported `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=`, preflight Step 0 saw the empty value and degraded to advisory (manual-invocation branch), push proceeded silently bypassing the gate. Root cause: design coupled the marker key to a stdin field the hook cannot guarantee. Fix: drop session_id from the marker key — the (commit sha) tuple alone is the right invariant (audit valid for the exact tree state; new commit invalidates; same code across sessions reuses the audit since identical bytes need no re-review). Hooks (meta + template) revert the .session_id extraction; preflight Step 0 drops the manual-invocation branch and blocks unconditionally on missing/stale marker when diff is governance-sensitive. SKILL.md Phase 0.5 marker spec updated to single-key. Bumps: framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/SKILL.md 1.1.0 → 1.1.1, framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/scripts/preflight.sh 1.1.0 → 1.1.1, framework_core.hooks/check-push-preflight.sh 1.1.0 → 1.1.1, templates.claude/hooks/check-push-preflight.sh 1.1.0 → 1.1.1. v2.9.0: feat(EVOL-025) Coherence Audit (Phase 0) + RDR Universal + context flag file. Three intertwined ships: (1) New `config/coherence-context.json` (single source of truth for `context: meta|downstream`; declares root_sets, exclusions, placeholder_allowlist_paths, lock_step_pairs, manifest_paths). Lives in meta as `framework_core.config/coherence-context.json` 1.0.0 and ships to downstream as `templates.config/coherence-context.json` 1.0.0 (target: `config/coherence-context.json`, materialised at SETUP --generate; SETUP --upgrade owns delta propagation since factory-sync.sh deliberately skips `config/`). Doubles as a generic context-detector that any future consumer can read. (2) New CLAUDE.md universal clause `## RDR Universal — MANDATORY` between Communication Style and Governance Scope, byte-identical between root and template per § What Lives Where. Universalises RDR application: any user-facing question with alternatives requires ≥3 options, justified recommendation, verbatim ratification, immediate persistence — no longer scoped only to SETUP/CODESIGN/BLUEPRINT/AUDIT/DEVOPS agent-posed decisions. Factual lookups (datum, not decision) keep the binary-question carve-out. Persistence path is context-specific (commit message / framework decision record in meta; IPP feature artefact in downstream). (3) factory-pr-review SKILL.md 1.0.1 → 1.1.0 gains Phase 0 Coherence Audit (agentic, semantic) executed BEFORE preflight Phases 1-6 when the diff is governance-sensitive. Phase 0 reads `config/coherence-context.json`, extracts changed symbols (renames, deletions, moved identifiers, public artefact names) from `git diff --name-status` + commit messages, builds a bounded collateral set via `git grep` against `audit.root_sets` minus `audit.exclusions`, and applies six new detectors: 13 Reference coherence (rename without lock-step propagation), 14 Placeholder leakage (`{{X}}` outside `audit.placeholder_allowlist_paths`), 15 Manifest ↔ disk coherence (entry pointing to non-existent path / file changed without manifest bump — inverse-direction of Block 11), 16-meta meta↔template lock-step (universal Factory-* and hooks/, governance-scripts, settings.json, CLAUDE.md universal clauses), 16-downstream code↔docs lock-step (rename_propagation + code_doc_lock_step pair types), 17 Commit ↔ diff coherence (claim ↔ content; EVOL-N branch ↔ EVOL-N commit), 18 Bump severity ↔ change kind (PATCH/MINOR/MAJOR ↔ feature/breaking/fix). On completion, Phase 0 writes a session+sha-scoped marker `.claude/state/coherence-audit-${session_id}-${branch_sha}.marker` with finding counts; new commit or new session invalidates the marker. Phase 0 is intentionally agentic — regex/grep heuristics for rename intent, lock-step semantics, and bump-severity judgment systematically miss true positives in framework work; the LLM call is paid only when the diff actually touches governance-sensitive paths and only once per (session, branch sha). (4) Push-gate scripts wired to enforce the marker: preflight.sh 1.0.1 → 1.1.0 gains Step 0 (after the docs-only fast-lane, before tool detection) that reads `config/coherence-context.json`, computes governance-sensitivity (any path under audit.root_sets minus exclusions), and on sensitivity looks for the marker file using `${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}` env var + `git rev-parse HEAD`. Missing marker → blocker `coherence-audit-missing` with humanised resolution path (run SKILL Phase 0). Marker present with `findings.blocker > 0` → blocker `coherence-audit-blockers`. Manual invocation (no `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`) degrades to advisory: log a note, do not block. Hook check-push-preflight.sh 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 extracts `.session_id` from stdin JSON alongside `.tool_input.command` and exports `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` before invoking preflight.sh, so Step 0 can locate the marker. Lock-step mirror at templates.claude/hooks/check-push-preflight.sh 1.0.0 → 1.1.0. (5) CLAUDE.md (root) 11.4.2 → 11.5.0 + claude/CLAUDE.md (template) 1.4.2 → 1.5.0 carry the new RDR Universal clause; new SKILL section + new Hard Blocks 13-18 documented in `framework_core.skills/Factory-pr-review/SKILL.md` 1.0.1 → 1.1.0 only (skill ships meta-direct via factory-sync.sh, no template counterpart). v2.8.3: fix(EVOL-024) factory-sync.sh 1.5.0 → 1.5.1 — strip trailing slash from `Factory-*/` glob expansion in sync_skills() before delegating to sync_tree(). Without the strip, the relative-path computation (${src_file#$src_root/}) failed and produced absolute paths in the destination, causing every Factory-* skill to show as `+ added` and would have been written to nested wrong locations. Caught by the Nexus-Tech-Link dry-run before applying. v2.8.2: feat(EVOL-024) factory-sync.sh 1.4.3 → 1.5.0 — sync_skills() now copies the FULL skill tree (SKILL.md + references/ + scripts/ + assets/ + README.md) via sync_tree, not just SKILL.md. Without this, `factory-sync.sh` skipped the new meta script when propagating to downstream projects, leaving them with the marker reader (governance-onprompt.sh 2.1.1) but no marker writer. Header documentation updated alongside. v2.7.1: fix(EVOL-023) Copilot PR #12 round 1 — three review findings addressed in lock-step on both governance-onprompt.sh and governance-onedit.sh: (a) marker-content allowlist filter — `.claude/state/` is user-writable so a tampered or legacy marker could carry quotes/`>`/newlines that would break the `<governance-source-edited paths="...">` tag or inject arbitrary content into the model-facing block; reader now filters with `awk '$0 == "docs/constitution.md" || $0 == "docs/setup.md"'` before interpolation, dropping anything unrecognised silently. (b) project-root anchoring guard — both hooks added an explicit `else exit 0` after the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR / git rev-parse cascade so the script no-ops when called from a CWD it cannot anchor (was previously falling through and writing markers / reading relative paths against the caller's CWD). (c) docs wording — freshness-gate header comment in onprompt rewritten from "always runs" to "runs by default, suppressed when the source-edit marker already attributed the cause" to match the actual control flow. Manifest: templates.scripts/governance-onprompt.sh 2.1.0 → 2.1.1, framework_core.scripts/governance-onprompt.sh 2.1.0 → 2.1.1, templates.scripts/governance-onedit.sh 1.0.0 → 1.0.1, framework_core.scripts/governance-onedit.sh 1.0.0 → 1.0.1. v2.7.0: feat(EVOL-023) governance source edit attribution + advisory freshness gate + bug fix. Three intertwined changes shipped together: (1) scripts/governance-onprompt.sh freshness gate switched from blocking (exit 2) to advisory (always exit 0, stdout `<governance-warning reason="snapshot-stale">` on stale) to remove the auto-livelock that occurred whenever an EVOL/ADR legitimately edited docs/constitution.md or docs/setup.md (the /setup carve-out only covered the recovery command itself, not the work that produced the staleness). (2) Fixes the unbound-variable bug introduced in the advisory rewrite: `${SCRIPT_DIR}` was never assigned, so under `set -u` the freshness subshell aborted before running validate-governance.sh, causing an empty warning block on every prompt regardless of actual snapshot state. (3) New PostToolUse hook scripts/governance-onedit.sh detects Edit/Write on docs/constitution.md or docs/setup.md and writes a session-scoped marker .claude/state/governance-source-edited-${session_id}.marker. governance-onprompt.sh, on the next prompt, reads the marker and emits a `<governance-source-edited paths="...">` block naming the edited paths and instructing the agent to regenerate the snapshot inline via factory-governance-loading SKILL § Step 1 POST-LOAD (does NOT require /setup --upgrade). When the source-edit block fires, the freshness gate is suppressed for that prompt — the agent already knows why the snapshot is stale. Net effect: edits to governance source no longer produce a generic stale-snapshot warning; instead the agent receives cause attribution + explicit regen instruction, breaking the prior livelock with traceable agent-driven regen. Wiring: .claude/settings.json + template gain a PostToolUse Edit|Write block invoking governance-onedit.sh. Manifest entry bumps: templates.scripts/governance-onprompt.sh 1.1.0 → 2.1.0 (2.0.0 advisory + 2.1.0 marker-aware), framework_core.scripts/governance-onprompt.sh 1.0.0 → 2.1.0 (same), templates.claude/settings.json 1.1.0 → 1.2.0 (PostToolUse entry added), new entries templates.scripts/governance-onedit.sh + framework_core.scripts/governance-onedit.sh at 1.0.0. v2.6.3: fix: factory-sync.sh 1.4.1 → 1.4.2 — PR #11 Copilot review. Usage message now lists --greenfield and --preserve-local flags (previously hidden). Orphan summary line keys off the DELETED counter instead of the HAS_ORPHANS flag — detect_orphans()/detect_tree_orphans() in hooks and templates categories increment DELETED but never touched HAS_ORPHANS, so 'No orphans detected' was being printed even when orphans existed. Header 'WHAT IT SYNCS' list realigned to .claude/hooks/*.sh to match the implementation (sync_dir uses `*.sh`, not `check-*.sh`). Spelling unified to 'materialized/materialization' across the error path. v2.6.2: fix: factory-sync.sh 1.4.0 → 1.4.1 — robustness fixes surfaced while syncing a greenfield target (Nexus-Tech-Link). (a) Greenfield targets were rejected because docs/setup.md was a hard prerequisite; now auto-detected when neither CLAUDE.md nor docs/project_log/ exist, or forced with --greenfield. (b) scripts/ category copied from $FRAMEWORK_ROOT/scripts/ directly, which leaked the meta-only auto-tag.sh (with governance-aware override) to downstream projects; now resolves each name to .context/templates/setup/scripts/<name> first, falling back to the meta path only when no template variant exists. (c) new --preserve-local flag that reports (PRESERVED counter) dsts that differ from source instead of overwriting them, for materialised projects with legitimate local modifications. (d) header documentation corrected (CLAUDE.md SKIPPED, factory-sync.sh synced). (e) global variables initialised at top to survive `set -u` regardless of flow path.

Same jq → python3 parser cascade as governance-onprompt.sh; degrades silently when both are absent (marker not written, agent falls through to plain freshness warning).

2.2.1: fix(IPP runtime binding) — adds Block 2b IPP marker consumer between source-edit attribution and the livelock carve-out. Two new model-facing tagged blocks emitted on stdout: `<ipp-reminder paths="...">` consumes `.claude/state/ipp-first-write-${session_id}.marker` (written by check-ipp-compliance.sh after a legitimate skeleton write) and emits Pillars 2/3 teaching text with SKILL pointer. `<ipp-warning reason="pillar-2-violation" entries="...">` consumes `.claude/state/ipp-pillar2-${session_id}.marker` (written by governance-onedit.sh Block 2 when filled sections outpace `_progress`) and emits a backfill instruction. Both markers session-scoped with legacy-unscoped fallback; consumed (deleted) after emission. Allowlist filters reject anything not under `docs/spec/*` or `docs/setup.md`. Freshness gate (Block 3) is NOT suppressed by IPP blocks — they are independent advisories. Lock-step with framework_core.scripts/governance-onprompt.sh.

2.1.1: fix(EVOL-023) Copilot PR #12 review: (a) marker-content allowlist filter — reader now drops anything that is not exactly `docs/constitution.md` or `docs/setup.md` before interpolating into the `<governance-source-edited paths="...">` attribute, closing an injection vector through a tampered/legacy marker file in the user-writable `.claude/state/` directory. (b) explicit `else exit 0` added after the project-root anchoring cascade so the hook no-ops when it cannot resolve a project root. (c) freshness-gate header comment rewritten from "always runs" to "runs by default, suppressed when the source-edit marker already attributed the cause" to match actual control flow.

When this block fires the freshness gate's `<governance-warning>` is suppressed for the same prompt — the agent already has cause attribution and an action.

2.0.0: BREAKING fix: freshness gate is now advisory, not blocking. On stale snapshot the hook emits a `<governance-warning reason="snapshot-stale">` block on stdout and exits 0 instead of exit 2. Removes the auto-livelock that occurred whenever an EVOL/ADR legitimately edited docs/constitution.md or docs/setup.md (the previous /setup carve-out only covered the recovery command, not the work that produced the staleness in the first place). The marker-replay path (post-compact re-inject of `<governance-reload>`) is unchanged. Downstream consumers depending on a non-zero exit code to halt their pipeline must read the stdout warning tag instead.

agent_templates._description: Per-role agent templates consumed by CODESIGN, BLUEPRINT (architect), DEVELOP, PO, QA, SECURITY, UX, and PEER REVIEW agents at feature time. Synced to materialised projects by factory-sync.sh under the same `.context/templates/{role}/` path. Tracked here so SETUP --upgrade can apply Smart Additive Merge with version awareness instead of factory-sync.sh's blind overwrite. Placeholders ({{FEATURE_ID}}, {{TIMESTAMP}}, etc.) are runtime-resolved by agents when generating per-feature artefacts — they are part of the template contract, not setup-time variables. Hence content_type: universal across the board.
agent_templates._architecture: Each entry's target is the same `.context/templates/{role}/{file}` path in the materialised project. SETUP --upgrade walks this section under Step 2 (Unified Smart Additive Merge) + Step 3 (New File Handling). EVOL-027 fase 1 — keystone for fase 5 (parser-canonical user_journey reaches MASS via --upgrade).

changelog: 2.5.3: fix(readme-mermaid-syntax) — § Complete Workflow Diagram failed to render on GitHub: every command-labeled node (21 occurrences) was written as `NodeId[/command --flag]`, which Mermaid parses as the parallelogram-shape opener `[/.../]` and chokes when no closing `/]` is found (lexical error on line 3). Wrapped every such label in quotes `NodeId["/command --flag"]` so Mermaid treats it as a plain rectangular node with the leading slash as literal content. No semantic change to the workflow.

Reads docs/spec/{FEATURE_ID}/increment_plan.md § 1 increments, computes overlap of each increment's scenarios_covered/contract_surface with the change's affected_scenarios/affected_contract_ops, and transitions status per-increment according to immutability policy: MERGED never invalidates (surfaces a follow-up-increment recommendation instead), BUILDING receives pending_iteration + --pause requirement (preserves in-flight work), {DRAFT, READY, INVALIDATED} flip to INVALIDATED with invalidated_by_iteration + invalidated_reason stamps. Plan-level status flips to INVALIDATED only when every non-MERGED increment is invalidated. Mirrors invalidated_increments[] into dev_plan.md frontmatter. skills/factory-pr-review/SKILL.md.role: Factory PR Review v1.1.0 — five-axis review (code, code↔docs, API contracts, ADR, traceability) wired as a LOCAL push gate (NOT a merge gate) plus an assistive PR reviewer for already-pushed branches. v1.1.0 adds Phase 0 Coherence Audit (agentic, semantic) executed BEFORE preflight Phases 1-6 when the diff is governance-sensitive. Phase 0 reads `config/coherence-context.json` (single source of truth for `context: meta|downstream`), extracts changed symbols (renames, deletions, moved identifiers, public artefact names), builds a bounded collateral set via `git grep` against `audit.root_sets` minus `audit.exclusions`, applies six new detectors (13 Reference coherence, 14 Placeholder leakage, 15 Manifest↔disk coherence, 16-meta meta↔template lock-step, 16-downstream code↔docs lock-step, 17 Commit↔diff coherence, 18 Bump severity↔change kind), and writes a session+sha-scoped marker `.claude/state/coherence-audit-${session_id}-${branch_sha}.marker`. Hard blocks total: 18 (12 from v1.0 + 6 new). Mode `--preflight` is the push-gate entry point invoked by the PreToolUse Bash hook; mode `--review {PR}` is manual and may publish to the platform with explicit user confirmation. Docs-only fast-lane allowlist matches CLAUDE.md Generation Standards §3 verbatim — every-path-in-allowlist exits 0 silently and skips Phase 0. Project-rule precedence documented in § Project rules and ADR precedence — the push gate consumes `branching.instructions.md`, `security_policy.md`, `testing.md`, `protected-code.md` + `protected-paths.json`, `codebase_inventory.json`, `contract-first-policy.md` + `api-standards.md`, `architecture.md`, `defect-prevention.md`, and `coherence-context.json` (Phase 0). Explicitly does NOT consume `review-policy.md` (BVL/IMPLEMENT 🔍 REVIEW hat scope, separate concern). ADR-driven exemptions via `pr_review_overrides:` frontmatter on ADRs under `docs/project_log/adr/` (project-wide) or `docs/spec/{ID}/adr/` (feature-scoped).

If the FINDINGS_FILE path contained spaces (rare with mktemp but possible), the literal would break. Now: export `PRE_FF="$FINDINGS_FILE"` as env var and read via `os.environ['PRE_FF']` only. Heredoc kept unquoted so other bash variables ($BASE_REF, $CURRENT, $CHANGED_FILES) continue to expand. Self-review mitigation during PR #16 audit. Out of scope (separate fix-PR): the `--json` mode `$CHANGED_FILES` multiline injection bug — pre-existing, unrelated to EVOL-025 path.

Replaced with `grep PATTERN FILE | wc -l | tr -d ' '`. Surfaced now because v1.1.0 added the 0-findings code path (governance-sensitive diff + present marker, no other blocker).

Marker keyed on commit sha alone — new commit invalidates; same tree across sessions reuses (identical bytes need no re-audit). Manual diagnostic invocation (`bash preflight.sh --json`) blocks the same way; users who need a non-blocking inspection can pre-write the marker. Lock-step with skills/Factory-pr-review/SKILL.md 1.1.1 + hooks/check-push-preflight.sh 1.1.1.

1.0.1: fix — split `local n="$1" path="...${n}..."` into two `local` statements in write_adr_proposed / flip_adr_to_accepted / write_fdr_proposed / flip_fdr_to_accepted. Bash evaluates ALL expansions on a single `local` line BEFORE any assignment, so `${n}` referenced the caller's (empty) `n` instead of the just-assigned local. Filenames rendered as `ADR--test.md` (empty number) on every scenario; tests passed locally only because the [adr-backfill] bypass at scenario 3 happened to mask the wrong filename. CI's gate exited 1 because the bypass did not match (subtle environment-dependent difference). Fix verified locally: paths now render as `ADR-003-test.md` correctly.

runtime_artefacts._description: Artefacts that materialise at SETUP --generate time WITHOUT a template source file. SETUP synthesises them from operational data (greenfield: empty seed; brownfield: scanned from existing codebase). Tracked here so SETUP --upgrade and CI can validate their PRESENCE in materialised projects without expecting a corresponding template source under `.context/templates/setup/`. Distinct from `templates` (which require source files) and `agent_templates` (which mirror agent-consumed scaffolds).
runtime_artefacts._architecture: Each entry's `target` is the synthesised location in the materialised project. `bootstrap_synthesised: true` flags that disk-coherence checks (factory-pr-review Block 15) skip the source-existence assertion for this entry. Versioning tracks the schema/format of the synthesised artefact, not source content.
